title: Vigna minima
intro: Vigna minima, also known as the small cowpea or wild cowpea, is a plant species belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae) that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and regulate blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Vigna minima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Vigna minima is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.

The classification of Vigna minima is as follows: it is a member of the genus Vigna, which includes other species of beans and peas, and it is further classified as Vigna minima (Burm.f.) O. Ktze.
